_notes: NBA scheduling constraints derived from the CBA, broadcast contracts, and arena operations.
The NBA uses proprietary optimization software (believed to be a variant of CP/MIP).
Key difference from EPL: back-to-back games are allowed but minimized; season is ~4x denser.

hard.description: Each team plays exactly 82 regular-season games
Division rivals x4, conference non-division x3-4, opposite conference x2

hard.source: NBA CBA — player health and safety
hard.description: No team may play three games in three consecutive nights

hard.source: Arena operations — teams sharing arenas with NHL/other events
hard.description: Teams sharing an arena cannot both play home games when the arena is unavailable

hard.source: NBA calendar — mandatory All-Star break, no games scheduled
hard.description: No games scheduled during All-Star break weekend

soft.source: NBA CBA / player welfare — back-to-back games are unavoidable but should be minimized
soft.description: Minimize the number of back-to-back game instances per team

soft.source: NBA player welfare — consecutive road back-to-backs are hardest on players
soft.description: Avoid scheduling back-to-back games where both are road games

soft.source: NBA team operations — long road trips disrupt player routines
soft.description: Teams should not go on road trips longer than 6 consecutive away games

soft.source: NBA scheduling standard — balanced home/away split per half-season
soft.description: Teams should have a roughly balanced home/away split in each half of the season (41H/41A)

soft.source: ABC/ESPN broadcast contract — Christmas Day is the highest-rated NBA regular season date
soft.description: Exactly 5 marquee games on Christmas Day; top-market teams must participate

soft.source: TNT/ESPN/ABC/NBC broadcast contracts — top-market teams drive ratings
soft.description: Top-market teams should appear in national broadcast windows proportionally

soft.source: NBA scheduling preference — spread rivalry matchups for sustained interest
soft.description: The two legs of high-profile rivalries should be spread across the season

soft.source: NBA/broadcast tradition — opening night should feature defending champion + marquee matchup
soft.description: Opening night (2 games) should include the defending champion as a participant

soft.source: NBA player welfare — transcontinental travel is significantly more fatiguing
soft.description: Minimize situations where teams cross more than 2 time zones on back-to-back nights

soft.source: NBA In-Season Tournament rules — group stage requires balanced home/away within group
soft.description: In-Season Tournament group stage games should alternate home and away within each group
